skill.name: ai-video-generation
skill.description: Generate AI videos with Google Veo, Seedance 2.0, HappyHorse, Wan, Grok and 40+ models via inference.sh CLI. Models: Veo 3.1, Veo 3, Seedance 2.0, HappyHorse 1.0, Wan 2.5, Grok Imagine Video, OmniHuman, Fabric, HunyuanVideo. Capabilities: text-to-video, image-to-video, reference-to-video, video editing, lipsync, avatar animation, video upscaling, foley sound. Use for: social media videos, marketing content, explainer videos, product demos, AI avatars. Triggers: video generation, ai video, text to video, image to video, veo, animate image, video from video, ai animation, video generator, generate video, t2v, i2v, ai video maker, create video with ai, runway alternative, pika alternative, sora alternative, kling alternative, seedance, happyhorse
skill.summary: Generate AI videos using 40+ models through the inference.sh CLI tool
skill.author: skills-shell

security_audit.summary: All 85 static analysis findings are false positives resulting from a markdown file being misinterpreted as executable code. The backtick patterns flagged as Ruby shell execution are markdown formatting (inline code and code blocks). The URLs flagged as network indicators are documentation references and example placeholders. The two high-severity 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings reference non-existent code in YAML frontmatter and a markdown table row. The skill's legitimate behavior executes the belt CLI via Bash, which is its documented and intended function.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in documentation
low_findings.description: 20 hardcoded URL instances detected in SKILL.md. All are false positives - they are documentation links to the inference.sh service, a CDN header image, and example placeholder URLs in code blocks. No actual network calls are made by the skill itself.

content.value_statement: Creating AI videos normally requires navigating multiple different tools and APIs. This skill gives you access to over 40 video generation models through a single CLI interface.
actual_capabilities: Generate videos from text descriptions using Veo, Seedance, HappyHorse, Grok, and WAN models
Create videos from images with Wan 2.5, Seedance 2.0, and HappyHorse I2V models
Produce AI avatar videos with lipsync using OmniHuman and Fabric models
Edit existing videos using natural language commands through HappyHorse Video Edit
Add sound effects to videos with HunyuanVideo Foley and merge clips with Media Merger
Browse and run over 40 AI video models through the inference.sh belt CLI
limitations: Requires the inference.sh belt CLI tool to be installed on your system
Video quality and generation speed depend on the specific model and API availability
Requires an active internet connection to access inference.sh API endpoints
Not all models support every feature such as audio generation or high resolution

use_cases.title: Social Media Video Creation
use_cases.description: Turn text prompts into short videos for social media platforms. Use Veo or Seedance to create engaging content without video editing software.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: Marketing Content Production
use_cases.description: Generate product demo videos, explainer animations, and marketing assets using text prompts. Edit and enhance videos with AI-powered tools.

use_cases.title: AI Avatar and Lipsync Videos
use_cases.description: Create talking head videos and animated avatars from a single image and audio file. Use OmniHuman or Fabric for realistic lipsync output.

prompt_templates.title: Simple text-to-video generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a video of a drone flying over a forest at sunset using Veo 3.1 Fast.
prompt_templates.scenario: New user creating their first AI video from a text description

prompt_templates.title: Image-to-video animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this image of a city street into a slow motion video using Wan 2.5. Use the image URL from my uploads folder.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to animate a static image into a short video clip

prompt_templates.title: Avatar creation with lipsync
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a talking avatar using this portrait image and this audio narration file. Use OmniHuman 1.5 for the best quality multi-character output.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs a video avatar for a presentation or educational content

prompt_templates.title: Multi-step video production workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: First generate a 10-second video of waves crashing on a beach using Seedance 2.0 with audio enabled. Then upscale the result with Topaz Upscaler. Finally add bird chirping sound effects using HunyuanVideo Foley.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user building a complete video production pipeline

output_examples.input: Generate a video showing a timelapse of a flower blooming in a garden using Veo 3.1 Fast.
output_examples.output: The skill runs the belt CLI command with the Veo 3.1 Fast model. The output is a short video file showing a flower transitioning from bud to full bloom, with smooth frame interpolation and optional ambient audio.

output_examples.input: Animate a portrait photo into a talking avatar using OmniHuman with the provided audio file.
output_examples.output: The skill processes the reference image and audio through the OmniHuman model. The output is a video of the portrait speaking with synchronized lip movements, natural head motion, and facial expressions matching the audio track.

output_examples.input: Edit this video by changing the background to a snowy mountain landscape using HappyHorse Video Edit.
output_examples.output: The skill sends the video and prompt to the HappyHorse Video Edit model. The output is the original video with the background replaced by a snowy mountain scene, while preserving the foreground subjects and their movements.

best_practices: Start with simple text prompts to test each model before adding complexity. This helps you understand the model strengths and output quality.
Use specific and detailed descriptions in your prompts. Clear prompts about camera movement, lighting, and subject action produce better videos.
Match the model to your specific use case. Use Veo for realistic footage, OmniHuman for avatars, and HunyuanVideo Foley for adding sound effects.
anti_patterns: Do not use placeholder URLs like https://your-image.jpg without replacing them with actual file paths to your images or videos.
Do not assume all models support the same features. Check each model documentation for input format, resolution limits, and audio support.
Do not chain too many models in a single workflow without testing each step individually first.

faq.question: What video models are available for text-to-video generation?
faq.answer: The skill supports Veo 3.1, Veo 3, Veo 2, Seedance 2.0, HappyHorse 1.0, Wan 2.5, and Grok Imagine Video for text-to-video generation.

faq.question: Can I add audio to the generated videos?
faq.answer: Yes. Some models like Veo 3.1 and Seedance 2.0 support built-in audio generation. You can also add sound effects separately using HunyuanVideo Foley.

faq.question: Do I need to install any software to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need the inference.sh belt CLI tool. Install it by running npx skills add belt-sh/cli. The belt CLI handles all API communication.

faq.question: What is the maximum video resolution available?
faq.answer: Resolution depends on the model. Seedance 2.0 and HappyHorse support up to 1080p. Wan and WAN models support up to 720p. Check each model for specific limits.

faq.question: Can I edit existing videos with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. HappyHorse 1.0 Video Edit supports natural language video editing. You can change backgrounds, modify scenes, and apply transformations using text prompts.

faq.question: How do I see all available video models?
faq.answer: Run belt app store --category video in your terminal to browse all 40+ available video generation and editing models.
